summary:
\(\boxed{\text{The central function of cholesterol in plasma involves transporting lipids primarily as part of LDL}}.\)
```
``` ```markdown
Cholesterol in plasma mainly serves to be transported efficiently throughout the body via Low-Density Lipoproteins (LDL), which accounts for about two-thirds of total cholesterol circulation. This critical task supports various cellular processes and is clinically significant given therapeutic strategies targeting LDL levels.
